instruction: Build a log rotation pipeline for application logs in /var/log/myapp/. (1) Create the directory /var/log/myapp/ and put three initial log files inside: app.log (at least 100 lines of text), error.log (at least 50 lines of text), and access.log (at least 30 lines of text). (2) Write a shell script /usr/local/bin/rotate_logs.sh that: a) for each .log file in /var/log/myapp/, renames it to <name>.log.1, compresses it with gzip to <name>.log.1.gz, b) creates a fresh empty .log file with permissions 644, c) moves all .gz files to /var/log/myapp/archive/. (3) Create the /var/log/myapp/archive/ directory with permission 755. (4) Run the rotation script once so that archive/ contains app.log.1.gz, error.log.1.gz, access.log.1.gz, and fresh empty .log files exist. (5) Create /var/log/myapp/rotation_report.txt containing the line 'rotated_count=3'.
source: 外部参考来源：Canonical tutorial - Command line for beginners (https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/command-line-for-beginners)

command: import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)

result.command: script_ok='false'; [ -x /usr/local/bin/rotate_logs.sh ] && script_ok='true'; archive_dir_ok='false'; [ -d /var/log/myapp/archive ] && [ "$(stat -c '%a' /var/log/myapp/archive)" = '755' ] && archive_dir_ok='true'; app_gz='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/archive/app.log.1.gz ] && app_gz='true'; error_gz='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/archive/error.log.1.gz ] && error_gz='true'; access_gz='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/archive/access.log.1.gz ] && access_gz='true'; fresh_app='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/app.log ] && [ ! -s /var/log/myapp/app.log ] && [ "$(stat -c '%a' /var/log/myapp/app.log)" = '644' ] && fresh_app='true'; fresh_error='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/error.log ] && [ ! -s /var/log/myapp/error.log ] && [ "$(stat -c '%a' /var/log/myapp/error.log)" = '644' ] && fresh_error='true'; fresh_access='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/access.log ] && [ ! -s /var/log/myapp/access.log ] && [ "$(stat -c '%a' /var/log/myapp/access.log)" = '644' ] && fresh_access='true'; report_ok='false'; [ -f /var/log/myapp/rotation_report.txt ] && grep -q 'rotated_count=3' /var/log/myapp/rotation_report.txt && report_ok='true'; echo "script_ok=$script_ok"; echo "archive_dir_ok=$archive_dir_ok"; echo "app_gz=$app_gz"; echo "error_gz=$error_gz"; echo "access_gz=$access_gz"; echo "fresh_app=$fresh_app"; echo "fresh_error=$fresh_error"; echo "fresh_access=$fresh_access"; echo "report_ok=$report_ok"
